current_wind.description: Light wind from the North-Northwest, with significant gusts.

wind_info.description: Headwind with a slight crosswind from the left

bh.disc_recommendation: Overstable driver or stable disc with hyzer flip.
bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Use a strong hyzer release to fight the headwind and the slight push to the right. A controlled hyzer flip might also work for a stable disc.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Consider if the push to the right is too much for RHBH control; a controlled anhyzer release can work, but backhand is often preferred for headwinds.

bh.disc_recommendation: Overstable driver or stable disc with anhyzer flip.
bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Use a strong anhyzer release to fight the headwind and the slight push to the left. A controlled anhyzer flip for a stable disc.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Consider if the push to the left is too much for LHBH control; a controlled hyzer release can work, but backhand is often preferred for headwinds.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Strong hyzer release to prevent turning over and stalling against the direct headwind. Backhand offers the best control here.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Generally less ideal for direct headwinds due to control challenges. If used, a very strong hyzer and overstable disc is needed.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Strong anhyzer release to prevent turning over and stalling against the direct headwind. Backhand offers the best control here.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Generally less ideal for direct headwinds. If used, a very strong anhyzer and overstable disc is needed.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Similar to Hole 2; use a strong hyzer release to combat the direct headwind.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Like Hole 2, a challenging angle for forehand into a direct headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Similar to Hole 2; use a strong anhyzer release to combat the direct headwind.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Like Hole 2, a challenging angle for forehand into a direct headwind.

wind_info.description: Slight headwind with a touch of crosswind from the left

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Throw flat or with a slight hyzer to manage the slight headwind and gentle push to the left.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A slight anhyzer release can counter the push to the right if needed, but backhand is generally preferred for control.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Throw flat or with a slight anhyzer to manage the slight headwind and gentle push to the right.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: A slight hyzer release can counter the push to the left if needed, but backhand is generally preferred for control.

wind_info.description: Tailwind with a significant crosswind from the right

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Use an anhyzer release to counter the crosswind pushing left, letting the tailwind provide extra glide. Excellent for distance.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: If the crosswind push left is too much for RHBH control, a RHFH with a hyzer angle can work, but may limit max distance.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Use a hyzer release to counter the crosswind pushing left, allowing the tailwind to provide extra glide. Good control for distance.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: If the crosswind push left is too much for LHBH control, a LHFH with an anhyzer angle can work, but may limit max distance.

wind_info.description: Headwind with a strong crosswind from the left

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: A strong hyzer release is crucial to combat both the headwind and the push to the right. Backhand offers better control in these conditions.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Can be used if you need to go around obstacles or if the crosswind push is too dominant, but control is harder.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: A strong anhyzer release is crucial to combat both the headwind and the push to the left. Backhand offers better control in these conditions.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Can be used if you need to go around obstacles or if the crosswind push is too dominant, but control is harder.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Overstable disc on a hyzer to fight the direct headwind. Backhand preferred.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Very difficult into a direct headwind, requires exceptional control.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Overstable disc on an anhyzer to fight the direct headwind. Backhand preferred.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Very difficult into a direct headwind, requires exceptional control.

wind_info.description: Tailwind with a crosswind from the right

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Use an anhyzer release to ride the tailwind and counteract the push to the left from the crosswind. Good for distance shaping.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Can be used to shape a shot around an obstacle or if the crosswind push is too significant for RHBH control. A hyzer angle helps.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Use a hyzer release to ride the tailwind and counteract the push to the right from the crosswind. Good for distance shaping.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: Can be used to shape a shot around an obstacle or if the crosswind push is too significant for LHBH control. An anhyzer angle helps.

wind_info.description: Crosswind from the left with a tailwind component

bh.disc_recommendation: Stable disc on hyzer or neutral disc with hyzer flip.
bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Fight the push to the right from the crosswind with a hyzer release or hyzer flip, leveraging the tailwind for distance. Backhand offers more control.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A hyzer release with a stable disc can help fight the rightward push, but it's generally more challenging to control than a RHBH in this scenario.

bh.disc_recommendation: Stable disc on anhyzer or neutral disc with anhyzer flip.
bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Fight the push to the left from the crosswind with an anhyzer release or anhyzer flip, leveraging the tailwind for distance. Backhand offers more control.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: An anhyzer release with a stable disc can help fight the leftward push, but it's generally more challenging to control than a LHBH in this scenario.
